provenance.source_corpus: On the Use of Shallow Basins to Restore Cutover Peatlands: Plant Establishment

source_spans.text: Results of these experiments demonstrate that shallow basins were generally effective at promoting Sphagnum establishment
Results of these experiments demonstrate that shallow basins were generally effective at promoting Sphagnum establishment
Soil-moisture content (linear positive effect) and duration and severity of flooding events (quadratic effect) were shown to be determinant of plant recovery.
duration and severity of flooding events (quadratic effect) were shown to be determinant of plant recovery.
moss cover, however, dropped when the likelihood of being subjected to frequent or severe flooding increased beyond a certain threshold
Initial mulch density had a linear positive impact on Sphagnum establishment
surface roughness (negative effect) were also instrumental in explaining local variation in Sphagnum cover.
active reintroduction of peatland plant diaspores... to improve the success of current peatland restoration techniques.
rewetting the abandoned peat surfaces by blocking the drainage ditches is a necessary step for restoration
rewetting the abandoned peat surfaces by blocking the drainage ditches is a necessary step for restoration

edges.original_sentence: The use of basins resulted in wetter conditions for peatland plants to develop; volumetric water content of surface peat averaged 88% in basins in 1996, significantly higher than the 71% measured for flat surfaces
